steps.title: Recognize the standard form of a circle equation
steps.content: This equation is in standard form, which directly gives us the center $(h, k)$ and radius $r$.

steps.title: Read off the center and radius
steps.content:
From $(x - 14)^2 + (y - k)^2 = 81$:
  • The $(x - 14)^2$ term shows the $x$-coordinate of the center is $14$
  • The $(y - k)^2$ term shows the $y$-coordinate of the center is $k$
  • The constant $81 = r^2$, so $r = \sqrt{81} = 9$
Center: $(14, k)$
Radius: $9$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
